title: Global News Weekly Roundup
subject: Global News Weekly Roundup — Week of 2026-03-28
summary: Iran war entered its second month with escalating strikes and counterstrikes across the Middle East
Oil surged past $110/barrel after Iran closed Strait of Hormuz to container ships
Colombian military plane crash killed 66; extreme weather hit Kenya and Tanzania
Markets tumbled for fifth straight week as energy disruptions rippled through global economy

stories.headline: Iran War Enters Second Month as Strikes Escalate
bullets: US extended deadline for Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz to April 6; Brent crude hit $110/barrel
Israeli Air Force struck uranium processing facility in Yazd and heavy water reactor at Khondab
10 US troops injured in Iranian strike on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia
G7 foreign ministers agreed to protect Strait of Hormuz passage if war ends
stories.framing_watch: RT emphasizes US aggression and civilian casualties; BBC and DW focus on diplomatic efforts and military operations

stories.headline: Kenya Flash Floods Kill 84; Tanzania Landslides Kill 20
bullets: At least 84 killed in flash floods across Kenya; 20 killed in landslides in Tanzania's Mbeya Region
Somalia faces acute food insecurity with 6.5 million affected as drought and conflict worsen

stories.headline: Colombian Military Plane Crash Kills 66
bullets: C-130 transport plane crashed in Puerto Leguízamo, killing at least 66 of 125 aboard
One of the deadliest military aviation disasters in Latin America in recent years

stories.headline: US Government Shutdown Drags On
bullets: House Republicans rejected Senate deal to fund DHS; TSA staffing shortages causing extended airport lines
Senators recessed for two weeks with no end in sight

stories.headline: EU Braces for Energy Crisis as Iran War Hits Economy
bullets: European Commission urged EU countries to start gas filling season early
Eurogroup warned conflict effects now passing through to real economy
Germany's Chancellor Merz questioned coal reform timeline given energy instability

stories.headline: Sarah Mullally Becomes First Female Archbishop of Canterbury
bullets: Enthroned on March 27, succeeding Justin Welby who resigned over safeguarding failures

stories.headline: Nepal Gets New PM as Former Leader Arrested
bullets: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah sworn in as PM after landslide election victory
Former PM KP Sharma Oli arrested over deadly crackdown that killed 70+ protesters

stories.headline: Markets Tumble as Oil Crisis Deepens
bullets: Dow fell ~800 points, entering correction; S&P 500 posted fifth straight losing week
OECD warned Middle East conflict is 'testing resilience of global economy'
Bank of England and Federal Reserve both held rates steady, citing energy-driven inflation

stories.headline: AI Deepfake X-Rays Fool Doctors; Grain-of-Salt Neural Implant
bullets: AI-generated fake X-rays now convincing enough to fool radiologists
New neural implant small enough to rest on a grain of salt can wirelessly transmit brain activity for over a year
Australian scientists demonstrated a prototype quantum battery

what_to_watch: Iran's April 6 Strait of Hormuz deadline — will Trump extend again or escalate?
US DHS shutdown with senators on recess for two weeks
Oil price trajectory as IEA considers further strategic reserve releases
